reason: Enabling FIPS mode involves changing kernel parameters and cryptographic policies. On a running system, this can break existing applications that rely on non-compliant cryptographic algorithms, cause service failures, or prevent the system from booting correctly if the kernel/bootloader configuration is not handled perfectly. The description explicitly states that enabling FIPS on a preexisting system is not supported via standard methods and requires specific boot-time configuration.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Kernel parameter changes can lead to boot failure; Cryptographic policy changes can break application compatibility; Non-compliant cryptographic modules will be disabled; System-wide impact on all security-sensitive services
